title: viola tricolor subsp. lutea
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. lutea, also known as the yellow pansy, is a subspecies of the heartsease plant, a member of the violet family characterized by its bright yellow and blue flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Viola tricolor subsp. lutea include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and support immune function. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
